PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-28-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 293563 The Mary Casserly Trust.

Appearances: Marilyn Young in Propria Persona. Mohammad Walizadeh for Co-Owners of property.

The Court expressed concern about the Trust not being executed by Ms. Casserly, a third Party signing the trust and then revoking his signature and the property never being placed in the Trust. Ms. Young stated that Trust was Ms. Casserly's idea prior to her health issues, that Father Hagan felt there was a conflict with him being a Trustee when he was also a Beneficiary with interest in the real property, that Mr. Ryan resigned as Trustee which left only her, that the property was not transferred into the Trust due to the decline in Ms. Casserly's health and that the focus was on Ms. Casserly's needs. Ms. Young also stated that she had a resolution with regard to Mr. Ryan's move out date. The Court denied the Petition to Confirm Trust Assets and granted the Petition for Probate (subject to review of the order). Bond is not required.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-07-2010 Continued To Jul-28-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jun-01-2010 Continued To Jul-07-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Marilyn Young in Propria Persona. Michael McNaughton for Patrick Casserly.

Ms. Young stated that the tenancy issue was still under discussion, that Mr. Ryan agreed that there was a conflict, that he verbally agreed to resign as Co-Trustee as of May 26, 2010 but that he had not signed anything, that she revised the petition to reflect that she is the sole Petitioner and the sole Trustee, and that she prepared a request to have the hearing date expedited. The matter was continued to July 7, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Special Letters of Administration were extended to July 7, 2010.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar May-18-2010 Continued To Jun-01-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Marilyn Young in Propria Persona. Michael McNaughton for Patrick Casserly, Stephanie Kiernan, Shane Donnigan, Michael Hickey, Kim McCauley and Kevin Hickey (Interested Parties). Irene Casserly and Rose Casserly also present.

The Court stated that the last time the matter was heard there was discussion as to whether there was a Trust or there was going to be a Hegstad petition, and that it had not seen a Trust document. Ms. Young stated that the Trust provisions were identical to the will with the omission of the $500.00 cash request to Covenant House, that she would ask the Court that the Trust be reformed and amended to include that gift and that the Trustees would be herself, Stephen Ryan and Reverend James Hagan. In addition, Ms. Young stated that Mr. Hagan has declined to serve, that Mr. Rocha (Counsel for Mr. Ryan) had court this morning and that there would be a conflict with Mr. Ryan being a tenant and a Co-Trustee. Mr. McNaughton objected to a Hegstad petition to the extent that it makes Stephen a Co-Trustee of an asset in which he was taking an adverse position to the Family claiming he is a disabled protected tenant. The matter was continued to June 1, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to see whether Mr. Ryan will resign as Co-Trustee. The Special Letters were extended to June 1, 2010. Ms. Young is to file a Trust petition, submit a declaration with regard to having the matter specially set for the Hegstad petition and advise the Court of the hearing date.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
